user.title: ⚡ Velkommen til CloudEMS v1.5
user.description:
**CloudEMS** optimises your energy usage fully locally — no cloud, no subscription.

**What CloudEMS does for you:**
- ⚡ Charge your EV and boiler at the cheapest time of day
- ☀️ Maximise self-consumption of solar power
- 📊 Monitor your quarter-hour peak for capacity tariffs
- 🔌 Automatically detect appliances via NILM
- 🚨 Warn about grid congestion in your area

This wizard takes about 5 minutes. You can rerun it via Settings → Integrations → CloudEMS → Reconfigure.

![]({diagram_url})

📖 [Documentation]({support_url}) · ☕ [Support CloudEMS]({buy_me_coffee_url}) · 🌐 [cloudems.eu]({website})

data_description.energy_prices_country: Select your country to fetch the correct EPEX day-ahead electricity prices.
data_description.wizard_mode: Basic: quick setup with essential sensors. Advanced: full control over all sensors, inverters and P1.
data_description.battery_sensor: ⚠️ Cloud batteries (e.g. Zonneplan) may have 30–300s delay. CloudEMS applies EMA smoothing automatically.
data_description.grid_sensor: Must be in Watts (W), not kWh or kW. P1 sensors typically report in W.
data_description.solar_sensor: Must be in Watts (W). Some inverters report in kW — use a template to multiply by 1000.

grid_connection.title: ⚡ Grid connection
grid_connection.description: Select the fuse size that matches your installation. This sets the maximum current per phase that CloudEMS will enforce.

data_description.phase_preset: E.g. 3×25A = 3-phase with 25 A per phase. Select 'Custom' to enter values manually.

phase_custom.title: ⚡ Custom current limits
phase_custom.description: Enter the maximum current allowed per phase. CloudEMS will never exceed these values.

data_description.max_current_l1: Maximum grid current on phase L1 — typically your fuse rating (e.g. 25 A).
data_description.max_current_l2: Maximum grid current on phase L2. Only relevant for 3-phase installations.
data_description.max_current_l3: Maximum grid current on phase L3. Only relevant for 3-phase installations.

grid_sensors.title: 🔌 Grid sensors
grid_sensors.description:
CloudEMS automatically found **{detected}** sensor(s) in your HA installation.

**Grid sensor (required):** One sensor measuring total grid power in W or kW. Positive = importing power, negative = exporting.

**Separate import/export:** Some integrations deliver import and export as two separate positive sensors. Choose this if your meter doesn't show negative values during export.

💡 **Tip:** Using a P1 reader (P1 Dongle, Homewizard, SLIMMELEZER)? Look for a sensor named something like `sensor.p1_power` or `sensor.net_power`.

![]({diagram_url})

data_description.use_separate_import_export: ⚙️ Use separate sensors if your meter measures import and export separately (e.g. DSMR5)
data_description.grid_sensor: 🔌 Single bidirectional sensor (positive = import, negative = export)
data_description.export_power_sensor: Sensor for grid export / feed-in power
data_description.power_sensor_l1_export: Einspeisung pro Phase L1 (nur DSMR5 / bidirektionaler Zähler). Leer lassen wenn nicht separat gemessen.

phase_sensors.title: ⚡ Fasesensorer
phase_sensors.description:
Phase sensors let CloudEMS monitor each of the {phase_count} phase(s) individually. Optional but strongly recommended for 3-phase connections.

**Current (A) per phase:** Measures current on each incoming cable using CT clamps installed in your meter cupboard.

**Benefits of phase monitoring:**
- Prevents a single phase from overloading during EV charging
- More accurate peak detection for capacity tariffs
- CloudEMS can automatically redistribute EV power across phases

💡 **Popular CT sensor hardware:** Shelly EM, Emporia Vue, iotawatt, Eastron SDM.

![]({diagram_url})

data_description.phase_sensors_L2: Current in amperes on phase L2.
data_description.phase_sensors_L3: Current in amperes on phase L3.
data_description.power_sensor_l1_export: Einspeisung pro Phase L1 (nur DSMR5 / bidirektionaler Zähler). Leer lassen wenn nicht separat gemessen.

solar_ev.title: 🌞 Solar & EV
solar_ev.description: Connect your solar inverter, battery and EV charger. All fields are optional.

data.solar_sensor: Solar inverter power sensor (W or kW)

data_description.solar_sensor: ☀️ Total PV power sensor (use sum sensor if you have multiple inverters)
data_description.negative_price_threshold: Price level below which solar curtailment activates. Default: 0.00 EUR/kWh.
data_description.gas_sensor: Link a gas meter for gas consumption analysis and heat pump COP.
data_description.gas_price_fixed: Fixed gas price in €/m³ if no live price sensor is available.

inverter_count.title: ☀️ Hvor mange invertere?
inverter_count.description:
Select the number of PV inverters in your installation. Select **0** to skip inverter management.

CloudEMS will self-learn azimuth, tilt and peak power from your production history.

📖 [Multi-inverter docs]({docs_url})

inverter_detail.title: ☀️ Inverter {inverter_num} of {total}
inverter_detail.description:
Configure inverter **{inverter_num}**.

**Power sensor:** The sensor measuring instantaneous PV output in W or kW. Usually found in your inverter integration (SolarEdge, Enphase, Solis, Huawei, etc.).

**Azimuth and tilt (optional but recommended):**
- Azimuth: panel direction. 0° = North, 90° = East, **180° = South**, 270° = West
- Tilt: angle from horizontal. 0° = flat roof, **30–35°** = typical pitched roof, 90° = vertical

💡 **{azimuth_tip}** — **{tilt_tip}**

Leave azimuth and tilt empty if unknown — CloudEMS will learn them automatically.

![]({diagram_url})

data.inv_azimuth: Azimuth (°) — optional, leave blank to self-learn
data.inv_tilt: Tilt angle (°) — optional, leave blank to self-learn
data.inv_rated_power: Rated inverter power (W) — optional, for accurate clipping detection

data_description.inv_sensor: 🔌 Connect the power sensor of this inverter (W or kW)
data_description.inv_rated_power: Peak rated power in Wp — leave 0 to let CloudEMS learn this automatically
data_description.inv_tilt: Tilt angle: 0 = flat roof · 30 = typical pitch · 90 = vertical
data_description.inv_control: Connect a switch or number entity to limit inverter output power

features.title: 🚀 Funktioner
features.description: Enable the features you need. You can change these at any time via Settings → Integrations → CloudEMS → Configure.

data.dynamic_loading: Dynamisk opladning — oplad EV i billigste timer
data.peak_shaving_enabled: Peak shaving — stay under monthly peak demand limit

data_description.dynamic_loading: Increase EV charging current during the cheapest EPEX hours and reduce it during expensive hours.
data_description.dynamic_load_price_threshold: Price at or below which an hour is considered 'cheap' for EV charging.
data_description.cost_tracking_enabled: Track daily, monthly and yearly energy costs using live EPEX prices.
data_description.phase_balance_enabled: Automatically redistribute loads to keep phase imbalance within the threshold.
data_description.phase_balance_threshold_a: Maximum allowed current difference between phases before rebalancing is triggered (A).
data_description.peak_shaving_enabled: Limit the monthly peak demand by switching off sheddable loads when demand exceeds the limit. Relevant for capacity-tariff countries (BE, FR).
data_description.price_alert_high_eur_kwh: Send an alert when the EPEX price exceeds this value. Default 0.30 €/kWh.
data_description.nilm_min_confidence: Devices with lower confidence are hidden in the dashboard. Default 0.65.

peak_config.title: 📊 Peak shaving settings
peak_config.description: Configure the peak demand limit and which loads CloudEMS may switch off to stay under it.

data.peak_shaving_assets: Sheddable loads (entities to switch off during peak)

data_description.peak_shaving_limit_w: Maximum allowed grid import power in Watt. When exceeded, CloudEMS will switch off sheddable loads.
data_description.peak_shaving_assets: Select switches, lights, or other controllable entities that may be turned off to reduce peak demand.

ai_config.title: 🤖 KI & NILM
ai_config.description:
CloudEMS can identify appliances from your power data (NILM — Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring).

**Priority:** Built-in pattern matching → Ollama (local) → Cloud AI

**None:** Pattern matching only, no external calls.
**Ollama:** Local LLM on your own hardware — privacy-first.
**CloudEMS / OpenAI / Anthropic:** Cloud AI for highest accuracy.

🔑 [Get a CloudEMS API key]({premium_url})

data.cloud_api_key: API key (required for CloudEMS, OpenAI and Anthropic)
data.nilm_min_confidence: Minimum confidence to accept a device detection (0.30–0.99)

data_description.ai_provider: Select which AI backend CloudEMS uses to identify appliances. Ollama and built-in are local; cloud options require an API key.
data_description.cloud_api_key: Enter your API key for the selected cloud provider. Leave blank when using None or Ollama.
data_description.nilm_min_confidence: Detections below this confidence are ignored. Lower = more results but more false positives. Default: 0.65.

ollama_config.title: 🤖 Ollama-indstillinger
ollama_config.description: Configure the Ollama server that CloudEMS will use for local AI inference.

data_description.ollama_host: Hostname or IP address of your Ollama instance.
data_description.ollama_port: TCP port Ollama listens on. Default is 11434.
data_description.ollama_model: Name of the Ollama model to use. Recommended: llama3, mistral or phi3 (smallest).

advanced.title: 📡 P1 smart meter
advanced.description: Optionally connect a P1/DSMR smart meter directly via TCP. Most users already have P1 data via the DSMR integration — only enable this if you want a direct TCP connection.

data_description.p1_host: IP address of your P1 gateway (e.g. HomeWizard P1, DSMR-reader).
data_description.p1_port: TCP port of the P1 gateway. Default is 8088.

battery_count.title: 🔋 Batterier — Antal
battery_count.description:
How many battery systems do you want to configure?

Each battery is configured individually with a power sensor, SoC and capacity.

prices.title: 💶 Priser og skat
prices.description: Configure energy tax and supplier markup for accurate cost calculations.

mail.title: 📧 E-mail reports (optional)
mail.description:
Configure e-mail to automatically receive monthly or weekly energy reports as PDF.

Leave empty to skip.

climate.title: 🌡️ Klimaat & Thermostaten
climate.description: CloudEMS kan thermostaten, TRV's en airco's intelligent aansturen op basis van EPEX-prijzen, zonne-overschot en aanwezigheid.

climate_boiler.title: 🔥 CV-ketel koppelen
climate_boiler.description: Koppel de entiteit waarmee CloudEMS de CV-ketel kan aansturen (switch, climate, of water_heater).

data.cv_min_zones: Minimum zones met warmtevraag om ketel aan te zetten

climate_zones.title: 🏠 Klimaatzones instellen
climate_zones.description: CloudEMS heeft de volgende klimaatentiteiten gevonden in uw installatie. U kunt zones later verfijnen via cloudems.configure_zone.

boiler_groups.title: 🚿 Warm Water Cascade
boiler_groups.description: Beheer meerdere boilers in groepen met slimme cascade-logica (sequentieel, parallel of prioriteit).

boiler_unit.title: 🚿 Boiler {boiler_num} van {total}
boiler_unit.description: Configureer de entiteit en instellingen voor deze boiler.

data.label: Label (display name)
data.energy_sensor: Power sensor (optional — CloudEMS learns wattage from this)

data_description.setpoint_c: Normal setpoint. During PV surplus, the maximum setpoint is used automatically to store as much solar energy as possible.
data_description.energy_sensor: CloudEMS learns the wattage automatically from this sensor. Without a sensor, 2500 W is assumed as default.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostik og notifikationer
diagnostics.description:
Configure e-mail to automatically receive monthly or weekly energy reports as PDF.

Leave empty to skip.

data_description.github_log_token: Token for automatic upload of anonymized diagnostic reports to GitHub. Create at github.com/settings/tokens with public_repo scope.
data_description.notification_service: Name of your HA mobile notify service for Guardian push notifications.

ha_energy_import.title: Sensorer fundet i Energi-dashboardet

data_description.grid_sensor: Single net power sensor: positive = import, negative = export.
data_description.import_power_sensor: Power consumed from the grid (always positive).
data_description.export_power_sensor: Power exported to the grid (always positive).
data_description.solar_sensor: Instantaneous PV power from your inverter.

managed_battery.title: Managed battery detected
managed_battery.description:
CloudEMS found {provider_count} vendor-managed battery integration(s): **{detected_providers}**.

These batteries are already controlled by the vendor. CloudEMS can supplement the control — opt-in only, so you stay in control.

You can change this later via Settings → Integrations → CloudEMS → Configure.

data_description.zonneplan_restore_mode: CloudEMS automatically restores the previous mode (e.g. Powerplay) after the set time.

shutter_count.title: 🪟 Roller Shutters — Count
shutter_count.description:
How many shutters do you want to connect?

{overkiz_found}

data.shutter_auto_solar_gain: Automatically open for solar heat gain

gas_opts.title: Gas & Heat
gas_opts.description: Gas consumption, boiler and heat pump settings

data.heat_pump_cop: Heat pump COP (manual, falls back to learned value)
data.heat_pump_thermal_entity: Heat pump thermal power sensor (W, optional)

abort.nilm_manage_via_services: Brug tjenesterne cloudems.rename_nilm_device og cloudems.hide_nilm_device i HA-udviklerværktøjer til at administrere enheder.

error.sensor_is_energy_not_power: Denne sensor måler energi (kWh), ikke effekt (W). Vælg en sensor med enhed W eller kW.

init.title: ⚙️ CloudEMS — Configure
init.description: Select the section you want to edit.

sensors.title: 🔌 Netsensorer
sensors.description: Grid connection, voltage and phase settings.

phase_sensors.title: ⚡ Fasesensorer
phase_sensors.description: Per-phase current, voltage and power. DSMR5 bidirectional meter: also fill in the export sensors for net phase power.

solar_ev_opts.title: ☀️ PV & EV Charging

data.gas_sensor: Gas meter sensor (m³) — optional, alternative to P1
data.gas_price_fixed: Fixed gas price (€/m³) — used when no sensor

solar_ev_opts.description: Solar panel sensor, EV charger and solar curtailment.

ai_opts.title: 🤖 KI & NILM
ai_opts.description: Select the AI provider and adjust NILM detection settings. NILM-detected devices appear as sensors under Settings → Devices → CloudEMS. You can confirm, rename or dismiss them there.

data_description.nilm_min_confidence: Detections below this confidence score are ignored. 0.65 is a good default.

data.outside_temp_entity: Outdoor temperature sensor (for heat demand mode)

data_description.congestion_threshold_w: Import power above which congestion is flagged. Typically your capacity tariff limit.
data_description.battery_chemistry: Select your battery chemistry. LFP degrades slower than NMC.
data_description.outside_temp_entity: Optional: link an outdoor temperature sensor. CloudEMS will prioritise heat pump / CV when cold and price is low.

inverter_detail_opts.title: ☀️ Inverter {inverter_num} of {total}

data.inv_azimuth: Azimuth (°) — optional, leave empty to self-learn
data.inv_tilt: Tilt angle (°) — optional, leave empty to self-learn

inverters_opts.title: 🔆 PV Inverters
inverters_opts.description: How many inverters do you want to configure? Current: {inverter_names}

batteries_opts.title: 🔋 Batteries
batteries_opts.description:
Currently configured: **{battery_names}** ({current_count} battery/batteries).

Set count to 0 to remove all. Increasing the count adds new batteries; decreasing removes the last ones.

battery_detail_opts.title: 🔋 Battery {battery_num} of {total}
battery_detail_opts.description:
Configure battery **{battery_num}**.

Leave capacity and power at 0 — CloudEMS will self-learn them.

prices_opts.title: 💶 Priser og skat
prices_opts.description:
Set whether energy prices are shown including tax and VAT.

The supplier markup is indicative — verify your own contract.

data.supplier_markup: Custom markup (€/kWh) — when 'Custom markup' is selected

nilm_devices_opts.title: 🏷️ NILM Apparaten beheren
nilm_devices_opts.description:
Hieronder staan alle gedetecteerde NILM-apparaten.

**Huidig overzicht:**
```
{devices_overview}
```

**Hernoemen** — gebruik de service `{rename_service}`:
- `device_id`: het ID tussen de haakjes hierboven
- `name`: nieuwe weergavenaam (max. 60 tekens)
- `device_type`: optioneel het type corrigeren

**Verbergen/tonen** — gebruik de service `{hide_service}`:
- `device_id`: het ID tussen de haakjes hierboven
- `hidden`: `true` om te verbergen, `false` om te tonen

👉 [Open Ontwikkeltools → Services]({devtools_url})

pool_opts.title: 🏊 Pool Controller
pool_opts.description: Configure the smart pool filter and heat pump. Leave fields empty if not used.

data_description.pool_filter_entity: Switch entity controlling the filter pump.
data_description.pool_heat_entity: Switch or climate entity controlling the heat pump.
data_description.pool_uv_entity: Optional: UV lamp for water disinfection.
data_description.pool_heat_setpoint: CloudEMS activates the heat pump when water temperature drops below this.

lamp_circ_opts.title: 💡 Lamp Circulation & Security
lamp_circ_opts.description: Configure intelligent lamp circulation. CloudEMS automatically cycles lights when nobody is home — for energy saving and burglary deterrence.

data.lc_use_sun_entity: Use sun entity for night detection (recommended)

data_description.lc_light_entities: Select all lights that may participate in circulation.
data_description.lc_excluded_ids: Lights never used for circulation (e.g. bedroom, bathroom).
data_description.lc_enabled: Enable or disable the entire lamp circulation module.
data_description.lc_use_sun_entity: Derive night from sun elevation — automatic seasonal adjustment.
data_description.lc_min_confidence: How certain CloudEMS must be that nobody is home. Higher = fewer false alarms.
data_description.lc_night_start_h: Fallback night start time if sun.sun is unavailable.
data_description.lc_night_end_h: Fallback night end time if sun.sun is unavailable.

cheap_switches_opts.title: ⚡ Cheap Hours Switches
cheap_switches_opts.description: Link up to 4 switches to the cheapest electricity block. CloudEMS turns them ON automatically. Leave a slot empty to skip it.

tab_visibility_opts.title: 👁️ Tab Visibility
tab_visibility_opts.description: Select which tabs you want to **hide** from the navigation bar. Hidden tabs are still accessible via direct URL but disappear from the tab row. CloudEMS applies this automatically in the Lovelace dashboard. The Overview tab is always visible.

boiler_group_add.title: ➕ Nieuwe cascade-groep
boiler_group_add.description: Geef de groep een naam en kies de cascade-modus.

boiler_group_edit.title: ✏️ Groep bewerken: {group_name}
boiler_group_edit.description: Pas de naam of modus aan van deze cascade-groep.

boiler_group_unit.title: 🚿 Boiler {unit_num} van {total} — {group_name}
boiler_group_unit.description: Koppel een HA-entiteit aan deze boiler-positie. Ondersteunde types: switch, climate, water_heater, number.

data.bu_energy_sensor: Power sensor (optional — CloudEMS learns wattage from this)
data.bu_surplus_setpoint: Max. setpoint at PV surplus / full battery (°C)

managed_battery_opts.title: Managed batteries ({provider_count} found)
managed_battery_opts.description:
Found: **{detected_providers}**

Adjust opt-in control settings. CloudEMS never permanently overrides vendor control.

climate_opts.title: 🌡️ Climate Management per Room
climate_opts.description:
Select the rooms for which CloudEMS should create a virtual thermostat. All climate devices in that HA room will be controlled together. Uncheck a room to remove its virtual device.

{discovery}

shutter_count_opts.title: 🪟 Roller Shutters — Count
shutter_count_opts.description:
How many shutters do you want to connect?

{overkiz_found}

data.shutter_auto_solar_gain: Automatically open for solar heat gain

data_description.shutter_night_close: Time at which the shutter closes automatically in the evening.
data_description.shutter_morning_open: Earliest time for automatic opening in the morning.
data_description.shutter_default_setpoint: Fallback when no thermostat is linked.

battery_type_opts.title: 🔋 Battery {battery_num} of {total} — Which type?
battery_type_opts.description:
Choose how CloudEMS controls this battery.

⚡ **Managed provider** (Zonneplan, Tibber…) — CloudEMS communicates via the official integration. No separate sensors needed.

🔧 **Manual** — You connect your own SOC sensor and charge/discharge switch.

{detected_hint}

cheap_switch_detail_opts.title: Switch {switch_num} of {total}
cheap_switch_detail_opts.description:
![Cheap Hours]({diagram_url})

**⚡ Cheap Hours Setting**
CloudEMS automatically turns this device ON during the cheapest price block.

---

**⏱️ Smart Delay Mode** *(optional)*
If you manually turn this device on during expensive electricity, CloudEMS will turn it OFF and automatically restart it during the cheapest hour.

data.sd_enabled: ⏱️ Enable Smart Delay Mode for this device

data_description.sd_enabled: Enable if this device (e.g. dishwasher, washing machine) is started manually and you want CloudEMS to wait for cheap electricity.
data_description.sd_power_sensor: A power sensor lets CloudEMS detect more reliably when the device starts.
data_description.sd_price_threshold_eur: Above this price CloudEMS turns the device off and waits for a cheaper hour.

smart_delay_opts.title: ⏳ Smart Delay Mode
smart_delay_opts.description:
![]({diagram_url})

**Smart Delay Mode** is the intelligent sibling of the cheap-hours switch.

**The difference:** with cheap-hours switches CloudEMS decides *when* the device starts. With smart delay mode *you start* the device yourself (e.g. press start on your washing machine), but if electricity is too expensive at that moment, CloudEMS automatically turns it **OFF** and restarts it as soon as the cheapest block begins.

---

**Typical devices:** 🧺 washing machine · 🍽️ dishwasher · 🌀 tumble dryer

---

You currently have **{smart_delay_count}** smart delay switch(es) configured.

Choose how many to set up *(0 = skip, only save cheap-hours switches)*.

smart_delay_detail_opts.title: ⏳ Smart Delay — device {switch_num} of {total}
smart_delay_detail_opts.description:
Configure when CloudEMS activates delay mode for this device.

---

⚡ **Price threshold** — CloudEMS turns the device OFF when EPEX price is *above* this level. Default €0.25/kWh — raise this if the device should almost always be allowed to run.

🔌 **Power sensor (optional)** — Link a power sensor so CloudEMS can verify the device is actually running. Without a sensor only the switch state is monitored.

⏱️ **Grace period** — Wait this many seconds after switch-on before delaying the device. Useful for devices that need a moment to initialise.

---

💡 *Tip: use the same earliest/latest hours as your cheap-hours switches for consistent scheduling.*

abort.nilm_manage_via_services: Brug tjenesterne cloudems.rename_nilm_device og cloudems.hide_nilm_device i HA-udviklerværktøjer til at administrere enheder.

errors.invalid_power_unit: Denne sensor har en ugyldig enhed. Brug en sensor der måler effekt i W eller kW.
